channel.title: Community Group for Sustainable Research in Computer Graphics
channel.description: Recent content on Community Group for Sustainable Research in Computer Graphics

item.title: Birds of a Feather SIGGRAPH 2026
item.description: Following the success of the first two BoFs at SIGGRAPH 2024 and 2025, we hope to once again create a space for researchers who want to think about the broader impacts of our research and how to collectively work towards a more sustainable future.

item.title: Birds of a Feather SIGGRAPH 2025
item.description: In 2025 the BoF event gathered about 25 people, and featured a structured group reflection activity. Attendees exchanged on topics of systemic issues in the current research landscape, shared methods to mitigate negative impact, and discussed ways to make attending conferences more sustainable.

item.title: Towards a sustainable use of GPUs in Graphics Research
item.description: We surveyed 888 SIGGRAPH papers from 2018-2024 and gathered author-reported GPU models. By contextualizing the hardware reported in papers with available data of consumers’ hardware, we demonstrate that research is consistently developed and tested on new high-end devices that do not reflect the state of the consumer-level market.

item.title: Birds of a Feather SIGGRAPH 2024
item.description: In 2024, we organized the first version of the Birds of a Feather event on The Environmental Impact of Computer Graphics. This one hour session was organized around two open discussions: (a) one about the impact of our domain, and (b) one about how what we create and develop in our field can help reducing the impact of other ones.
